message:
feat(cli): add --robot-spawn for structured session creation (ntm-oln)

Implements a new root-level flag for programmatic session creation with
structured JSON output, useful for orchestration and automation.

Features:
- New robot/spawn.go with SpawnOutput struct and PrintSpawn function
- Flags: --robot-spawn, --spawn-cc, --spawn-cod, --spawn-gmi
- Optional --spawn-wait for agent readiness detection with timeout
- --spawn-no-user to skip user pane creation
- --spawn-preset for future recipe integration

Output includes:
- Session name, created_at timestamp, working directory
- Per-agent details: pane ID, type, title, ready status, startup_ms
- Total startup timing

Also includes hooks integration in spawn command (pre/post-spawn events).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"ntm-a8q","title":"Implement --robot-ack for send confirmation","description":"Track and confirm message receipt by agents.

## Rationale
After --robot-send, I need to know:
1. Did the agent receive the message?
2. Did the agent acknowledge understanding?
3. Is the agent actively working on it?

Without this, I'm blind after sending - I have to poll tail output and parse for confirmation patterns.

## Proposal: Ack Tracking
When sending with --robot-send --track, ntm:
1. Watches pane output for acknowledgment patterns
2. Returns structured confirmation

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "sent_at": "ISO8601",
  "confirmations": [
    {
      "pane": "0.1",
      "ack_type": "prompt_returned",  // agent showed ready prompt
      "ack_at": "ISO8601",
      "latency_ms": 234
    },
    {
      "pane": "0.2",
      "ack_type": "echo_detected",    // agent echoed input
      "ack_at": "ISO8601",
      "latency_ms": 156
    }
  ],
  "pending": ["0.3"],  // still waiting
  "failed": []
}
```

## Ack Detection Patterns
- prompt_returned: Agent shows ready prompt after processing
- echo_detected: Input echoed in pane (basic confirmation)
- explicit_ack: Agent responds with understood/acknowledged
- output_started: Agent begins producing output

## Timeout Handling
--timeout=30s - wait up to 30s for acks

 {"id":"ntm-a9nk","title":"Enable UBS dogfooding: run scans on NTM codebase","description":"## Overview
NTM should eat its own dog food by using UBS to scan its own Go codebase, demonstrating the integration and catching real issues.

## Background
Per AGENTS.md: 'ubs <changed-files> before every commit. Exit 0 = safe.'

If NTM integrates UBS, it should use it on itself. This:
- Proves the integration works on real code
- Catches bugs in NTM's Go code
- Provides example output for documentation
- Builds confidence in the tooling

## Implementation

### CI Integration
- Add UBS scan step to any CI/CD pipeline
- Fail PR if critical issues found
- Cache UBS installation for speed

### Developer Workflow  
- Document in README: 'Run `ntm scan .` before commits'
- Or just `ubs .` until ntm scan is implemented
- Add to CONTRIBUTING guidelines (if we had one)

### Initial Scan
- Run full scan on current codebase
- Triage and fix any critical/error findings
- Document false positives for future reference

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Initial full scan completed
- [ ] Critical issues fixed or documented as false positives
- [ ] CI integration (if applicable)
- [ ] Developer workflow documented","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:39:09.927684-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:52:19.449596-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T03:52:19.449596-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-a9nk","depends_on_id":"ntm-lk66","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:39:19.512658-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-aqqa","title":"Implement pre-commit hook for UBS quality gate","description":"## Overview
Create a pre-commit hook that runs UBS scans and blocks commits containing critical issues, ensuring code quality standards are maintained.

## Background
Pre-commit hooks provide the last line of defense before code enters the repository. Integrating UBS here:
- Catches issues before they're committed
- Provides immediate feedback to developers/agents
- Enforces quality standards consistently

## Implementation Details

### Hook Behavior
1. On commit, run UBS on staged files only (fast path)
2. Parse results and check against configurable thresholds
3. Block commit if thresholds exceeded
4. Output clear, actionable error messages

### Configuration (.ntm.yaml or similar)
```yaml
scan:
  pre_commit:
    enabled: true
    block_on:
      critical: true  # Block on any critical
      error: 5        # Block if >5 errors
      warning: false  # Don't block on warnings
    timeout: 30s
    exclude:
      - "*.test.go"
      - "vendor/**"
```

### Hook Installation
- `ntm hooks install` - Install pre-commit hook
- `ntm hooks uninstall` - Remove hook
- Hook script delegates to `ntm scan --staged --strict`

### Output Format
```
🚫 Commit blocked by NTM quality gate

Found 2 critical issues in staged files:

  src/auth/login.go:45 [critical] SQL injection vulnerability
  src/api/handler.go:123 [critical] Unhandled error return

Run 'ntm scan --staged' for details.
To bypass: git commit --no-verify (not recommended)
```

### Files to Create/Modify
- internal/hooks/precommit.go - Hook logic
- internal/hooks/install.go - Hook installation
- internal/cli/hooks.go - hooks subcommand
- scripts/pre-commit.sh - Actual hook script template

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Hook installs correctly in git repo
- [ ] Only staged files are scanned (fast)
- [ ] Configurable blocking thresholds
- [ ] Clear error messages with file:line references
- [ ] Bypass option documented but discouraged","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteStone","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:33:34.385746-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:56:22.357164-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T03:56:22.357164-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-aqqa","depends_on_id":"ntm-p77j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:34:59.453887-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-aqqa","depends_on_id":"ntm-lk66","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:35:16.639902-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-iws","title":"Implement --persona flag for spawn/add","description":"# Implement --persona Flag for spawn/add

## Purpose

Allow spawning agents using persona definitions.

## Syntax

```bash
ntm spawn myproject --persona=architect --persona=implementer:2
ntm add myproject --persona=reviewer
```

## Flag Parsing

```go
type PersonaSpec struct {
    Name  string
    Count int  // Default 1
}

func ParsePersonaSpec(value string) PersonaSpec {
    parts := strings.SplitN(value, ":", 2)
    spec := PersonaSpec{Name: parts[0], Count: 1}
    if len(parts) > 1 {
        spec.Count, _ = strconv.Atoi(parts[1])
    }
    return spec
}
```

## Persona Resolution

```go
func ResolvePersona(name string) (*Persona, error) {
    // 1. Check project personas (.ntm/personas.toml)
    // 2. Check user personas (~/.config/ntm/personas.toml)
    // 3. Check built-in personas
    // 4. Return error if not found
}
```

## Spawn Integration

When spawning with --persona:
1. Resolve persona definition
2. Map agent_type to spawn flag (--cc, --cod, --gmi)
3. Set model from persona
4. Prepare system prompt for injection
5. Create pane with persona name in title

## Pane Naming with Persona

```
myproject__cc_architect_1
myproject__cc_implementer_1
myproject__cc_implementer_2
```

## Compatibility with Other Flags

```bash
# Persona + extra count
ntm spawn myproject --persona=architect --cc=2:sonnet

# Persona overrides model
ntm spawn myproject --persona=architect  # Uses opus from persona
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] --persona flag parsed in spawn command
- [ ] --persona flag parsed in add command
- [ ] Persona resolved from config
- [ ] Agent type and model derived from persona
- [ ] Pane names include persona
- [ ] Error messages for unknown personas
- [ ] Works alongside explicit --cc/--cod/--gmi flags","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:23:47.899357-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:23:47.899357-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-iws","depends_on_id":"ntm-hzl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:24:00.719552-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-iws","depends_on_id":"ntm-5zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:24:00.82547-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-jar","title":"EPIC: Output Intelligence & Search","description":"Search, extract, diff, and archive agent outputs intelligently.

## Problem Statement
Agent outputs are trapped in tmux pane buffers with limited tooling:
- Can only capture point-in-time snapshots via `ntm save`
- No search across historical outputs
- Code blocks in agent responses must be manually copied
- Can't compare responses when multiple agents answer the same question
- No persistent archive of valuable agent outputs

## Goals
1. Enable regex search across all pane buffers (`ntm grep`)
2. Parse and extract code blocks from agent markdown output
3. Compare outputs when sending same prompt to multiple agents
4. Create persistent, searchable output archives

## Success Criteria
- `ntm grep 'pattern'` searches all panes with context
- `ntm extract` pulls code blocks with file path detection
- `ntm diff` shows side-by-side agent response comparison
- Output logs are timestamped and searchable

## Technical Approach
- Leverage existing CapturePaneOutput() for buffer access
- Implement markdown code fence parser for extraction
- Use diff algorithm for output comparison
- JSONL-based output logging with rotation

## Code Block Extraction
```
Agent output:
Here's the implementation:
\`\`\`python
# src/auth.py
def authenticate(token):
    return validate(token)
\`\`\`

Extracted:
- Language: python
- Path hint: src/auth.py
- Content: def authenticate...
```

## User Value
- Find information across all agents quickly
- Extract and apply code without manual copy/paste
- Make informed decisions by comparing agent approaches
- Build searchable knowledge base from agent interactions","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:37:21.823529-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:37:21.823529-05:00"}
 {"id":"ntm-jar.1","title":"Output Search (ntm grep)","description":"Search across all pane output buffers with regex support.

## Command Interface
```bash
ntm grep 'pattern' myproject          # Search all panes
ntm grep 'error' myproject --cc       # Only Claude panes
ntm grep 'def.*auth' myproject -i     # Case insensitive
ntm grep 'TODO' myproject -C 3        # 3 lines context
ntm grep 'pattern' myproject -n 100   # Search last 100 lines
ntm grep 'pattern' --all              # Search all sessions
```

## Output Format
```
myproject/cc_1:42: def authenticate(user, token):
myproject/cc_1:43:     if not validate(token):
myproject/cc_1:44:         raise AuthError("Invalid token")
--
myproject/cod_1:15: # TODO: Add authentication middleware
```

## Implementation
```go
func GrepPanes(session, pattern string, opts GrepOptions) ([]Match, error) {
    re, err := regexp.Compile(pattern)
    if err \!= nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid pattern: %w", err)
    }
    
    panes, err := tmux.GetPanes(session)
    if err \!= nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    var matches []Match
    for _, pane := range panes {
        if \!matchesFilter(pane, opts.Filter) {
            continue
        }
        
        output := tmux.CapturePaneOutput(pane.ID, opts.Lines)
        lines := strings.Split(output, "\n")
        
        for i, line := range lines {
            if re.MatchString(line) {
                matches = append(matches, Match{
                    Session: session,
                    Pane:    pane.Title,
                    Line:    i + 1,
                    Content: line,
                    Context: getContext(lines, i, opts.Context),
                })
            }
        }
    }
    
    return matches, nil
}
```

## Flags
| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| -i | Case insensitive |
| -v | Invert match |
| -C N | Show N lines context |
| -A N | Show N lines after |
| -B N | Show N lines before |
| -n N | Search last N lines (default: all captured) |
| -l | List matching panes only |
| --cc, --cod, --gmi | Filter by agent type |
| --json | Output as JSON |

## Color Output
- Match highlighted in yellow/bold
- Pane name in blue
- Line numbers in grey
- Context separator (--) in dim

## Performance Considerations
- Capture is O(lines) per pane
- Pattern matching is O(chars)
- For large outputs, consider streaming
- Limit default capture to last 1000 lines

## Files to Create
- internal/cli/grep.go - Command implementation
- internal/output/search.go - Search logic
- internal/output/match.go - Match data structures","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:42:21.59932-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:42:21.59932-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-jar.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-jar","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:42:21.59983-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-jar.2","title":"Code Block Extraction","description":"Parse markdown code blocks from agent output for easy extraction.

## Problem
Agents often output code in markdown format. Manually copying code blocks is tedious and error-prone.

## Command Interface
```bash
ntm extract myproject                  # Extract from all panes
ntm extract myproject cc_1             # From specific pane
ntm extract myproject --last           # From most recent active pane
ntm extract myproject --lang=python    # Only Python blocks
ntm extract myproject --copy           # Copy to clipboard
ntm extract myproject --apply          # Apply to files (with confirmation)
ntm extract myproject --json           # Output as JSON
```

## Output
```
Found 3 code blocks in myproject/cc_1:

[1] python (detected: src/auth.py)
    ┌────────────────────────────────────────
    │ def authenticate(user, token):
    │     """Authenticate user with token."""
    │     if not validate(token):
    │         raise AuthError("Invalid")
    │     return get_user(token)
    └────────────────────────────────────────

[2] python (new file)
    ┌────────────────────────────────────────
    │ class AuthService:
    │     def __init__(self, secret):
    │         self.secret = secret
    └────────────────────────────────────────

[3] bash (command)
    ┌────────────────────────────────────────
    │ pytest tests/ -v
    └────────────────────────────────────────

Actions: [c]opy [a]pply [s]kip [q]uit
```

## Code Block Detection

### Markdown Fence Parsing
```go
type CodeBlock struct {
    Language  string   // From fence (python, bash, etc.)
    Content   string   // The code itself
    StartLine int      // Where in output
    FilePath  string   // Detected file path (if any)
    IsNew     bool     // Appears to be new file vs modification
}

func ParseCodeBlocks(output string) []CodeBlock {
    // Match 
    re := regexp.MustCompile("(?ms)^```(\\w*)\n(.*?)^```")
    matches := re.FindAllStringSubmatch(output, -1)
    
    var blocks []CodeBlock
    for _, m := range matches {
        block := CodeBlock{
            Language: m[1],
            Content:  m[2],
        }
        block.FilePath = detectFilePath(block.Content)
        blocks = append(blocks, block)
    }
    return blocks
}
```

### File Path Detection
Look for hints in the code or surrounding context:
```go
func detectFilePath(content string) string {
    // Check first line for path comment
    // # src/auth.py
    // // path/to/file.js
    // <!-- file.html -->
    
    // Check for module/package declarations
    // package main -> could be main.go
    // from fastapi import -> *.py
    
    return "" // If can't determine
}
```

## Apply Mode
When --apply is used:
1. For each block with detected path:
   - Show diff if file exists
   - Confirm before writing
2. For blocks without path:
   - Prompt for destination
   - Or skip

```
Apply [1] to src/auth.py?
  --- existing
  +++ new
  @@ -15,4 +15,8 @@
    def authenticate(...)
  +     if not validate(token):
  +         raise AuthError
  [y]es [n]o [d]iff [e]dit [s]kip all: 
```

## Interactive Mode (default)
If no action flags, enter interactive mode:
- Arrow keys to navigate blocks
- Enter to select action
- c=copy, a=apply, s=skip

## Files to Create
- internal/cli/extract.go - Command
- internal/output/codeblock.go - Parsing logic
- internal/output/apply.go - File application","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:42:55.483856-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:42:55.483856-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-jar.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-jar","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:42:55.485658-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-nxf","title":"Integrate context status into dashboard","description":"# Integrate Context Status into Dashboard

## Purpose

Show context usage in the dashboard view for at-a-glance monitoring.

## Dashboard Enhancement

Add context bar to each pane card:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Panes (4)                                                   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ● cc_1   opus      working    [████████░░] 62%              │
│ ● cc_2   sonnet    idle       [█████████░] 89% ⚠️           │
│ ● cod_1  gpt4      working    [███░░░░░░░] 31%              │
│ ● gmi_1  pro       idle       [░░░░░░░░░░]  5%              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Implementation

Add context field to pane model in dashboard:
```go
type PaneCard struct {
    // Existing fields...
    ContextUsed int
    ContextMax  int
    ContextPct  float64
}
```

Render context bar:
```go
func (p PaneCard) ContextBar(width int) string {
    filled := int(p.ContextPct * float64(width) / 100)
    empty := width - filled
    
    color := theme.Green
    if p.ContextPct > 80 {
        color = theme.Red
    } else if p.ContextPct > 60 {
        color = theme.Yellow
    }
    
    return color.Render(strings.Repeat("█", filled)) + 
           theme.Dim.Render(strings.Repeat("░", empty))
}
```

## Refresh Strategy

- Context data cached for 60s
- Press 'c' to force context refresh
- Background refresh if dashboard open >60s

## Keyboard Shortcut

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| c | Refresh context data |
| C | Open full context view |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Context bar displayed per pane
- [ ] Color coding (green/yellow/red)
- [ ] Warning icon for >80%
- [ ] Keyboard shortcut for refresh
- [ ] Graceful handling when data unavailable","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:26:13.013354-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:26:13.013354-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-nxf","depends_on_id":"ntm-jda","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:26:23.526527-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-oba","title":"Implement --robot-terse for ultra-compact output","description":"Single-line encoded state for extremely token-constrained scenarios.

## Rationale
Sometimes I need just the essentials in minimum tokens. A full snapshot might be 500+ tokens; terse mode gives me the critical state in <50 tokens.

## Usage
```bash
ntm --robot-terse
ntm --robot-snapshot --terse  # terse snapshot
```

## Output Format
```
S:myproject|A:3/3|R:30|B:27|I:3|M:5|!:2
```

Decoded:
- S: Session name
- A: Agents (active/total)
- R: Ready beads
- B: Blocked beads  
- I: In-progress beads
- M: Unread mail
- !: Alert count

## Multi-Session
```
S:proj1|A:2/2|R:15|B:10|I:1|M:2|!:0;S:proj2|A:3/3|R:20|B:5|I:2|M:0|!:1
```

## Use Cases
1. Polling in tight loops
2. Embedding in prompts with strict token limits
3. Quick health checks
4. Dashboard status bars

## Parsing
Provide parsing utilities in robot package:
```go
func ParseTerse(s string) *TerseState
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:52:39.009743-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:52:39.009743-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-oba","depends_on_id":"ntm-sim","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:39.818091-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

## Rationale
Current spawn returns human-readable output. For orchestration, I need structured confirmation of exactly what was created.

## Usage
```bash
ntm --robot-spawn myproject --preset=fullstack
ntm --robot-spawn myproject --cc=2 --cod=1
```

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "created_at": "ISO8601",
  "preset_used": "fullstack",
  "working_dir": "/path/to/project",
  "agents": [
    {"pane": "0.0", "type": "claude", "ready": true, "startup_ms": 234},
    {"pane": "0.1", "type": "claude", "ready": true, "startup_ms": 198},
    {"pane": "0.2", "type": "codex", "ready": true, "startup_ms": 312}
  ],
  "layout": "tiled",
  "total_startup_ms": 512
}
```

## Ready Detection
- Parse pane output for agent prompt/ready indicator
- Timeout after configurable wait (default 30s)
- Report ready=false with reason if agent didn't start

 {"id":"ntm-ozcj","title":"EPIC: Deep CASS Integration - Cross-Agent Session Search","description":"Transform NTM from a session manager into an intelligent AI agent orchestration platform by deeply integrating CASS (Coding Agent Session Search). This enables agents to learn from past sessions, avoid duplicate work, surface institutional knowledge, and provide operators with actionable insights on agent activity.

## Vision
CASS indexes 10+ AI agent conversation histories into a unified, sub-50ms searchable index. Combined with NTM's session management:
- Before solving: Query CASS for similar issues → avoid re-solving
- While working: Surface relevant context from past sessions  
- After solving: New session auto-indexed for future reference
- For operators: Dashboard showing agent activity, trends, insights

## Architecture
- internal/cass/ - Go client for CASS robot mode
- ntm cass <subcommand> - CLI commands for search, status, insights
- --robot-cass-* flags - Machine-readable output
- TUI widgets - CASS search in dashboard

## Success Metrics
- Agents can query CASS in <100ms
- 30% reduction in duplicate problem-solving
- Operators see unified agent activity view","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:27.867341-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:39:02.250344-05:00"}
 {"id":"ntm-ozcj.1","title":"CASS Infrastructure: Create internal/cass Go client package","description":"Create the foundational Go client package for CASS integration.

## Overview
This package provides a Go interface to CASS robot mode, enabling NTM to query past agent sessions, check health, and retrieve insights.

## Files to Create
- internal/cass/client.go - Main client with exec wrapper
- internal/cass/types.go - Response types (SearchHit, Status, Capabilities, etc.)
- internal/cass/search.go - Search API wrapper
- internal/cass/health.go - Health check integration
- internal/cass/timeline.go - Timeline API wrapper
- internal/cass/client_test.go - Unit tests with mocks

## Key Design Decisions

### Execution Model
Use os/exec to call cass CLI (not HTTP) because:
- CASS is a local tool, not a server
- Robot mode is designed for CLI consumption
- No need for daemon/socket infrastructure

### Error Handling
- CASS exit codes map to Go errors
- Retry on transient errors (timeout, busy)
- Graceful degradation when CASS not installed

### Response Types
Mirror CASS robot mode JSON schemas:
```go
type SearchHit struct {
    SourcePath  string  `json:"source_path"`
    LineNumber  *int    `json:"line_number,omitempty"`
    Agent       string  `json:"agent"`
    Workspace   string  `json:"workspace"`
    Title       string  `json:"title"`
    Score       float64 `json:"score"`
    Snippet     string  `json:"snippet"`
    CreatedAt   *int64  `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
    MatchType   string  `json:"match_type"`
    Content     string  `json:"content,omitempty"`
}

type SearchResponse struct {
    Query        string       `json:"query"`
    Limit        int          `json:"limit"`
    Offset       int          `json:"offset"`
    Count        int          `json:"count"`
    TotalMatches int          `json:"total_matches"`
    Hits         []SearchHit  `json:"hits"`
    Meta         *Meta        `json:"_meta,omitempty"`
    Aggregations *Aggregations `json:"aggregations,omitempty"`
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Client detects if CASS is installed
- [ ] Search returns parsed results
- [ ] Health check works
- [ ] Timeout and retry logic
- [ ] All error codes mapped
- [ ] Tests pass with mock CASS output","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:49.800387-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:42:47.427671-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T03:42:47.427671-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:49.800822-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
